message:
perf(core,vdbe,btree,e2e): defer MemDatabase row mirroring to read/commit boundaries for lazy dirty tracking

Previously, :memory: connections inside explicit transactions applied
MemDatabase row mutations immediately per-row, causing redundant work
for write-heavy workloads where intermediate states are never read.
This change queues row deltas in pending_memdb_direct_upserts and
flushes them lazily only at read, savepoint, or COMMIT boundaries.

- Replace resolve_autocommit_txn_with_capture_and_cx with new
  resolve_autocommit_txn_with_dirty_table_and_capture_and_cx that
  accepts optional dirty_table_name, consolidating retained autocommit
  dirty-table marking into the resolve function across all call sites
- Extend defer_memdb_upsert to fire for ALL explicit transactions
  (not just file-backed), with deferred-then-flush-on-boundary semantics
- Fix to_ascii_lowercase -> to_lowercase for dirty table name matching
- Adjust scratch buffer capacity test to allow growth on first reuse
  (learning the record-header envelope), assert stability from third
  execution onward
- Add btree cursor tests: hot-set seek avoidance, interpolation vs
  binary search equivalence on dense keys, zero-read cached sequential
  appends before split
- Add VDBE-level lazy dirty tracking tests: insert flag, read-from-btree
  fallback, multi-table isolation, update-after-insert, delete-after-insert
- Add comprehensive e2e tests: 120-row mixed DML with oracle comparison,
  10k sequential insert correctness, cross-statement visibility within
  explicit transactions, file-backed retained autocommit with close/reopen
  durability, sequential vs interleaved BEGIN CONCURRENT append/seek ratios
- Update legacy SQLite submodule pointer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
